authoritative_sources.title: Implementing Health IT
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC/ASTP is the federal health IT policy office. Its SAFER guidance directly supports EHR implementation, configuration, validation, maintenance, APIs, integration issues, patient identification, and downtime contingency expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: application and integration intake; change-readiness and downtime prerequisite validation
used_for: EHR configuration and maintenance evidence expectations
system-to-system API and integration safety framing
patient identification and downtime contingency dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Using Health IT
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC/ASTP SAFER material is authoritative for health IT safety practices involving EHR use, order entry, test results reporting and follow-up, clinician communication, training, and workflow risks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident triage and patient-safety escalation for orders, results, communication, and workflow failures

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Security Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR administers HIPAA Security Rule guidance for electronic protected health information safeguards and security management expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: access request, PHI-sensitive ticket, audit, and security-exception triage

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.312 - Technical safeguards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for HIPAA technical safeguards, including access control, audit controls, integrity, person or entity authentication, and transmission security.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IAM, access-control, audit, and secure-transmission boundary handling
used_for: generic account or MFA bypass refusal

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI use and disclosure in healthcare operations and support communications.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-sensitive support artifact handling and stakeholder communication

authoritative_sources.title: Information Blocking
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC/ASTP information-blocking guidance is authoritative for EHI access, exchange, and use policy involving health care providers, health IT developers, health information networks, and exchanges.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: system access, data exchange, EHI availability, and access denial boundary classification
used_for: boundary between support triage and legal/regulatory interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Health Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS provider-facing EHR reference describes EHRs as provider-maintained records containing administrative and clinical data and supporting care-related activities through interfaces.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: system-of-record and EHR workflow truth-set identification
used_for: EHR as patient and clinical-administrative source context

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS final-rule guidance is authoritative for payer/provider interoperability and prior-authorization API expectations that influence access to authorization, eligibility, and payer data dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, authorization, payer API, and referral-interface dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Preparedness and Response Capabilities for Health Care Coalitions
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASPR is the federal preparedness and response agency for health care readiness. Its capabilities guidance supports continuity, coordination, response, and recovery dependencies for healthcare system outages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: downtime, outage, continuity, and high-severity operational escalation

authoritative_sources.title: FHIR Release 4 Resource Index
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is the recognized standards body for FHIR. The resource index grounds healthcare interface terminology across patient, encounter, diagnostic, request, financial, provenance, and audit resources.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: interface terminology normalization and integration evidence mapping
used_for: orders, results, claims, charge, coverage, audit, and provenance terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Health Information Technology Hazard Manager
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and safety agency. The health IT hazard work supports discovery, impact, urgency, correction, and mitigation of health IT hazards.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient-safety impact classification and unresolved hazard escalation

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include ITSM ticket, requester identity, application owner, environment, affected healthcare workflow, integration points, access role when applicable, patient-safety impact, PHI sensitivity, severity, downtime or operational-continuity impact, source-of-truth hierarchy, approval path, change/test/rollback evidence, privacy/security policy, payer/referral/authorization context when relevant, and responsible owner acceptance.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Patient or member context is anchored by EHR encounter, MPI/patient identity, registration, clinical documentation, and EHR application administration records. IT support truth is anchored by ITSM, EHR admin, integration engine, IAM, monitoring/observability, vendor case, downtime, audit, and change-management systems. Claim, charge, eligibility, or authorization context depends on revenue-cycle, payer portal, clearinghouse, referral, and authorization systems. Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy controls conflicts.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical triage, clinical workflow content approval, medication or order decisions, privacy disclosure approval, security/access exception approval, payer coverage or medical-necessity interpretation, authorization approval, coding or claim disposition, release approval, vendor contract authority, and unauthorized production changes.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requester, environment, application, affected workflow, symptom, severity, PHI posture, access role, patient-safety impact, downtime impact, payer or revenue dependency, and intended action before treating a support request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and system-of-record retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve ITSM ticket details, EHR application records, integration engine logs, IAM status, monitoring alerts, vendor cases, downtime procedures, change records, privacy/security approvals, and revenue/payer dependency records.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Fulfillment or troubleshooting disposition
workflow_stages.description: Classify incident, access, interface, downtime, vendor, or change-readiness state; determine suspected component and operational impact; identify whether the lane can produce a support disposition or must escalate.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Stakeholder communication and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Prepare stakeholder-safe status, outage, or resolution communication with minimum-necessary PHI handling and traceability to authoritative records, owner acceptance, residual risks, and closure criteria.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Policy, authority, repeat-failure, and safety escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate clinical workflow changes, patient-safety risks, PHI handling uncertainty, access/security exceptions, payer interpretation, untested changes, downtime owner gaps, conflicting systems, and repeat interface failures.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: payer portal, eligibility, referral, and authorization status records
systems_of_record: EHR patient, encounter, order, result, medication, and documentation records
revenue-cycle, charge, claim, eligibility, referral, authorization, and payer portal systems
decision_boundaries: May classify incidents by application, interface, access, workflow impact, severity, and patient-safety sensitivity.
May determine whether a change or fix is blocked on testing, rollback evidence, approval, downtime procedure update, communication plan, or patient-safety review.
May route access blockers to IAM/security/privacy owners but may not approve access or exceptions.
May summarize interface evidence and suspected component but may not make unauthorized mapping, data correction, or production changes.
May identify payer, eligibility, referral, authorization, charge, or claim dependencies but may not interpret payer policy or approve coverage.
May escalate clinical workflow impact but may not make diagnosis, treatment, medication, order, or clinical workflow content decisions.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Patient or member harm from missed order, result, medication, downtime, or clinical workflow escalation.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require patient-safety impact capture, clinical operations route, owner assignment, and no clinical workaround invention.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI exposure through overbroad tickets, screenshots, distribution lists, logs, or vendor packets.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Apply minimum-necessary handling, approved channels, privacy escalation, audit note, and sanitized artifact request.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Unauthorized access, generic-account use, MFA bypass, or RBAC exception treated as support convenience.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Refuse exception, preserve IAM/security owner route, emergency-access policy requirements, and audit trail.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Production change bypasses testing, rollback, patient-safety review, or release approval.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Block change readiness, record missing evidence, and escalate to release/change and clinical safety owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Revenue leakage or claim denial from unresolved charge, claim, eligibility, authorization, or referral interface failure.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Capture revenue-cycle impact, payer/dependency records, owner route, and no payer interpretation.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Incomplete documentation trail or unsupported closure of an incident.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require ITSM ticket, systems checked, evidence links, communication summary, owner acceptance, residual risk, and closure criteria.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Conflicting public source, tenant policy, vendor instruction, or system-of-record state is synthesized into a false confident answer.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Record ambiguity, apply stricter tenant dependency when applicable, and return to orchestrator or responsible owner.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Stale eligibility and prior authorization records block scheduling interface resolution and require payer/patient-access escalation.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Broad EHR access and PHI-heavy screenshot distribution are controlled through RBAC, minimum-necessary, audit, and privacy escalation.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Clinical workflow content changes, payer authorization interpretation, downtime affecting medication/order flow, and clinical validation dependencies are routed outside the lane.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: An interface failure breaks orders, charges, or results flow across systems
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Orders, results, and charges are each represented with healthcare interface evidence and escalation or closure rules.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: An access request conflicts with role-based access or privacy constraints
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: RBAC/minimum-necessary access conflict and generic-account/MFA bypass workaround are refused or escalated.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: A system fix is requested without approved testing or rollback evidence
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Untested production fix and public-baseline versus tenant clinical-validation conflict both block change-readiness.
Clinical workflow changes and security exception workarounds are outside lane authority.
Repeated charge interface failures are escalated with incident history, owner routing, and conflicting evidence.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Request for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Production change shortcut and generic-account/MFA bypass are refused.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Scenarios use CPOE, ORM messages, results backlog, vendor case, interface reconciliation, ITSM, and EHR application artifacts.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Boundary rejection when request should move to adjacent specialist
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Routes to privacy, IAM/security, clinical informatics, payer/patient access, release/change, and clinical owners.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Missing environment, owner, workflow, integration point, access role, and patient-safety impact prevent disposition.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Vendor/interface conflict and public-baseline versus tenant-policy conflict require escalation.

required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.required_family: Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves public baseline and records assumptions
required_domain_scenario_family_mapping.coverage_notes: Runtime facts, tenant source hierarchy, stricter tenant policy, and assumptions are captured before action.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: Tenant-specific EHR, IAM, privacy, downtime, and release policies
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Spec requires retrieval and owner confirmation at runtime; validated baseline does not guess tenant approval thresholds.

unresolved_ambiguity.topic: State, facility, payer, and vendor-specific rules
unresolved_ambiguity.handling: Spec requires orchestrator return when these rules conflict with public baseline or materially change disposition.
Spec requires tenant escalation SLA retrieval and treats absent SLA as a low-confidence blocker.

research_to_spec_constraints: Every support disposition must include systems-of-record evidence and cannot rely on generic troubleshooting language.
Orders, results, medication, downtime, and clinical workflow impacts trigger patient-safety escalation instead of improvised operational workaround.
Access and PHI requests are constrained by RBAC, minimum-necessary, audit, and privacy/security owner approval.
Change-readiness requires testing, rollback, approval, communication, downtime, and patient-safety review evidence before production action.
Eligibility, authorization, referral, claim, and charge issues can be identified as dependencies but not decided as payer or reimbursement interpretation.
Conflicting public, tenant, vendor, payer, or system records require escalation or orchestrator return.

source_classes_covered: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
